description:
Goal: the work graph should expose the behaviors the fixpoint design depends on without doing structural type work in the engine. Signal: FactPattern is gone, waits are exact-key O(1), AppliedStep reports changed/enqueued/coalesced/blocked work from one seam, and semantic type comparisons happen through stable interned type ids that can be memoized inside the type system. Strategy: delete speculative wait-pattern generality, return complete applied-step data from Scheduler::complete, emit cheap raw telemetry from World::complete_job, and make convergence safety lean on fact-value changes plus InternedConcreteTypes comparison caches rather than formatting, structural hashing, or ad hoc fingerprints at every compiler layer.

Change:
- Replace FactPattern/ExactPattern with exact FactKey wait maps.
- Extend AppliedStep with coalesced and blocked work.
- Emit raw job/fact/coalesced/blocked events from World::complete_job using AppliedStep; handlers may format if they care.
- Add the narrow InternedConcreteTypes memoization seam needed for repeated subtype/equivalence/disjointness checks over immutable type ids.
- Keep convergence guards at the work-graph/fact boundary: repeated changed facts are signal, but type comparison caching belongs in the type context.

Why:
- Waits are exact today; the trait and linear scan are unused generality on a hot path.
- The engine already knows what changed and what work moved; Compiler2 should observe that uniformly instead of sprinkling special events.
- Interned type ids are stable integers. Once a comparison between two ids is known, the result is stable for the lifetime of the type context and should be memoized there, not rediscovered through structural Ty hashing in Compiler2.

Checks:
- Scheduler tests cover exact waits, coalesced follow-ups, blocked waits, and changed facts.
- InternedConcreteTypes tests cover memoized repeat comparisons returning the same answers without rewalking structure.
- Compiler2 trace-summary uses AppliedStep events as its daily signal.
- A higher-order recursive non-convergence repro emits a fatal diagnostic naming the unstable fact if the fact graph still churns after type comparison caching.
title: Compiler2 engine: exact waits, applied-step signal, and interned-type convergence
